content:
# Recommendations — after session 94 (iter-094)

## Highest priority — OPEN THE K1+S2 FILE-SPLIT LANE (committed progress-critic watch)
- B1 closed this iter. The committed watch (recorded iter-094 plan) is: **open the K1+S2 file-split lane the iter immediately after B1 closes.** Deferring it a 3rd time would be STUCK-by-inaction.
- **K1** `pullbackTensorMap_isIso_of_isOpenImmersion` (TensorObjInverse L806) + **S2** `tensorObj_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` (L988): both carry `set_option maxHeartbeats 6400000` and **OOM in place** on the 2 GB host (Exit 137, demonstrated iter-091). They must be **split into their own leaf module** (a `refactor` lane) so the heavy proof elaborates in isolation, THEN filled.
- Both are now mathematically UNBLOCKED: K1 by iter-093's re-ported `isIso_oplaxδ_of_conj` (v4.30 recipe `117100c4`:TensorObjSubstrate:4790); S2 by the now-closed B1 (its proof substitutes Bridge B1).
- **Serialization:** the file-split `refactor` lane edits `TensorObjInverse.lean`; do NOT co-run a `prove` lane on the same file (ARCHON_MEMORY build-race rule). Refactor first (insert `sorry` at the split boundary, green build), then `prove` the split-out leaf.

## Fix the 2 stale B1 docstrings (do it on the K1 iter — they touch the same region)
- `TensorObjInverse.lean` **L784–786**: claims `pullbackTensorMap_isIso_of_isOpenImmersion` is "cited from TensorObjSubstrate.lean L4770" (implying proven), directly above its own local `:= sorry` stub + TODO. Contradiction — update to state K1 is a local WIP sorry pending the file-split fill.
- `TensorObjInverse.lean` **L800–803**: B1 STATUS comment names a now-proven lemma (`sheafPullbackUnit_forget_eq`) as "the only transitive residual"; the actual transitive sorry is **K1**. Update the STATUS to name K1.
- These are `.lean` comments (review agent cannot edit). Hand to the prover/refactor on the K1 lane. (lean-auditor `iter094`, majors #2 #3.)

## Do NOT retry / do NOT re-flag
- **K1/S2 statement-block `\leanok` is NOT laundering** — lvb `iter094` reported it as a must-fix; it is a FALSE POSITIVE (statement-block `\leanok` = "formalized, sorry present", which is correct). Verified proof blocks carry no `\leanok`; sync ±0 is correct. Future reviews: do not strip these and do not re-flag.
- **K1/S2 must NOT be filled in place** (without the file-split) — OOM-kill is certain (iter-091). No prover should retry an in-place fill.

## Deferred (later phase — informal proof first)
- **Dual-unit FLANK** (`dual_unit_iso_restrict_assemble` L1481, `trivialisation_restrict_compat` L2245, `dualUnitIso_dualIsoOfIso` L2327) + **terminal** `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (L2382). All gated on the ∞-source **`lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one`** (`archon dag-query gaps` count 1 — the sole ∞ hole, unchanged). It has NO informal proof ⇒ send **dag-walker/blueprint-writer** to write a finite-effort informal proof BEFORE any prover. Do not assign a prover to the FLANK until this is done.

## Coverage debt (standing — for the post-recovery cleanup phase)
- `archon dag-query unmatched` = **154** `lean_aux` nodes with no blueprint entry (unchanged from iter-093; B1 added no new helper). The engine is still mid-migration (helper set unstable until the K1/S2 split lands), so blueprinting a moving target remains churn. Clear after the migration completes (helpers frozen; mark genuinely-private helpers `private` off the scan). The dead-dup `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (TensorObjSubstrate L716) deletion also belongs to that cleanup.

## Blueprint doctor
- iter-094 report: **clean** (no orphan chapters, no broken `\ref`/`\uses`, no new `axiom`). Nothing to address.
